diagrams.title: Producer-Consumer Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows producer threads generating data, shared bounded buffer with synchronization primitives, and consumer threads consuming data
elements: Producer Thread(s) → Shared Buffer ← Consumer Thread(s)
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate the overall architecture and data flow in producer-consumer problem

diagrams.title: Circular Buffer State Machine
diagrams.description: Three-state diagram showing buffer transitions: Empty ↔ Partially Filled ↔ Full, with producer and consumer actions triggering transitions
diagrams.purpose: Visualize valid buffer states and transitions for synchronization correctness

diagrams.title: False Sharing Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows CPU cache lines and how adjacent buffer slots in different cache lines vs same cache line affect producer-consumer performance
elements: CPU Core 1 (Producer) cache line with Slot 0
CPU Core 2 (Consumer) cache line with Slot 1
Case A: Slots aligned to cache lines - no false sharing
Case B: Slots share cache line - false sharing causes invalidation
Memory bus traffic arrows showing unnecessary transfers in Case B
diagrams.purpose: Explain performance impact of data layout on cache behavior
